command_log.command: mkdir -p .wheel/outputs/team-test && echo 'Team static test started' > .wheel/outputs/team-test/setup.txt && echo 'Setup complete'
echo "Workflow started. Hooks are now active."

steps.output:
## Static Team Test Results

Summary:
null
null

Collected outputs:
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-1/assignment.json
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-1/context.json
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-1/output.md
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-2/assignment.json
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-2/context.json
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-2/result.md
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-3/assignment.json
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-3/context.json
.wheel/outputs/team-test/results/worker-3/result.md

command_log.command: echo '## Static Team Test Results' && echo '' && echo 'Summary:' && cat .wheel/outputs/team-test/summary.json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.completed_count, .failed_count' && echo '' && echo 'Collected outputs:' && find .wheel/outputs/team-test/results/ -type f 2>/dev/null | sort

# Check if team-wait completed automatically
for f in .wheel/state_*.json; do
  wf=$(jq -r '.workflow_name' "$f" 2>/dev/null)
  [[ "$wf" == "team-static-test" ]] || continue
  jq -c '{cursor: .cursor, teammates: [.teams["create-team"].teammates | to_entries[] | {n:.key, s:.value.status}]}' "$f"
done || echo "No parent state — workflow may have completed entirely"
